name: SpriteMultitouchJoystick

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Detect presses made on a touchscreen on the object so it acts like a button and automatically trigger the button having the same identifier for the mapper behaviors.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: MultitouchButton

eventsFunctions.name: IsJustPressed
eventsFunctions.description: Check if the button was just pressed.

eventsFunctions.name: IsPressed
eventsFunctions.description: Check if the button is pressed.

eventsFunctions.name: IsReleased
eventsFunctions.description: Check if the button is released.

eventsFunctions.name: IsButtonJustPressed
eventsFunctions.description: Check if a button was just pressed on a multitouch controller.

eventsFunctions.name: IsButtonPressed
eventsFunctions.description: Check if a button is pressed on a multitouch controller.

eventsFunctions.name: IsButtonReleased
eventsFunctions.description: Check if a button is released on a multitouch controller.

eventsFunctions.name: IsDirectionPushed4Way
eventsFunctions.description: Check if joystick is pushed in a given direction.

eventsFunctions.name: IsDirectionPushed8Way
eventsFunctions.description: Check if joystick is pushed in a given direction.

eventsFunctions.name: StickForce
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the force of multitouch contoller stick (from 0 to 1).

eventsFunctions.name: StickAngle
eventsFunctions.description: Return the angle the multitouch controller stick is pointing towards (Range: -180 to 180).

eventsFunctions.name: StickForceX
eventsFunctions.description: Return the multitouch contoller stick force on X axis (from -1 at the left to 1 at the right).

eventsFunctions.name: StickForceY
eventsFunctions.description: Return the multitouch contoller stick force on Y axis (from -1 at the top to 1 at the bottom).

eventsFunctions.name: HasTouchStartedOnScreenSide
eventsFunctions.fullName: New touch on a screen side
eventsFunctions.description: Check if a new touch has started on the right or left side of the screen.

eventsFunctions.name: TeleportAndPress
eventsFunctions.description: Show the joystick until it is released.

eventsFunctions.name: ActivateControl
eventsFunctions.description: De/activate control of the joystick.

eventsFunctions.name: IsPressed
eventsFunctions.description: Check if a stick is pressed.

eventsFunctions.name: StickForce
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the strick force (from 0 to 1).

eventsFunctions.name: StickForceX
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the stick force on X axis (from -1 at the left to 1 at the right).

eventsFunctions.name: StickForceY
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the stick force on Y axis (from -1 at the top to 1 at the bottom).

eventsFunctions.name: StickAngle
eventsFunctions.description: Return the angle the stick is pointing towards (from -180 to 180).

eventsFunctions.name: IsDirectionPushed4Way
eventsFunctions.fullName: Joystick pushed in a direction (4-way movement)
eventsFunctions.description: Check if joystick is pushed in a given direction.

eventsFunctions.name: IsDirectionPushed8Way
eventsFunctions.fullName: Joystick pushed in a direction (8-way movement)
eventsFunctions.description: Check if joystick is pushed in a given direction.

eventsFunctions.name: ControllerIdentifier
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the multitouch controller identifier (1, 2, 3, 4...).

eventsFunctions.name: SetControllerIdentifier
eventsFunctions.description: Change the multitouch controller identifier (1, 2, 3, 4...).

eventsFunctions.name: JoystickIdentifier
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the joystick name of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetJoystickIdentifier
eventsFunctions.description: Change the joystick name of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: DeadZoneRadius
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the dead zone radius of the joystick (range: 0 to 1). The deadzone is an area for which movement on sticks won't be taken into account (instead, the stick will be considered as not moved).

eventsFunctions.name: SetDeadZoneRadius
eventsFunctions.description: Change the dead zone radius of the joystick (range: 0 to 1). The deadzone is an area for which movement on sticks won't be taken into account (instead, the stick will be considered as not moved).

description:
Multitouch joysticks are objects showing a joystick on the screen, useful for mobile. They work like a physical gamepad:
- 4 or 8 directions
- Analogus pads
- Player selection
- Automatic "mapper" behaviors for 2D and 3D movement behaviors (platformer characters, top-down movement, 3D character etc...). Add one of these to the object with the movement behavior (i.e: the player most of the time) and the behavior will then be controlled automatically by the virtual joystick (it works by reading the multitouch joystick state and simulating controls). No need for additional events to make it work once the behaviors are set up.

There are ready-to-use joysticks in the asset-store [multitouch joysticks pack](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?initial-dialog=asset-store&asset-pack=multitouch-joysticks-multitouch-joysticks).
